source: Bluetooth Core Specification, Vol 1, Part F (Controller Error Codes)

0x00.name: Success
0x00.description: The command or procedure completed without error.

0x01.name: Unknown HCI Command
0x01.description: The controller does not recognize the HCI command opcode that was sent.
common_causes: Host sent an opcode the controller firmware does not implement
Version mismatch between host stack and controller firmware

0x02.name: Unknown Connection Identifier
0x02.description: A command was sent referencing a connection handle that the controller has no record of.
common_causes: Stale connection handle used after disconnect
Race between disconnect and a command targeting the old handle

0x03.name: Hardware Failure
0x03.description: The controller has detected a hardware failure that prevents it from completing the requested action.
common_causes: Faulty radio/controller chip
Firmware crash

0x04.name: Page Timeout
0x04.description: BR/EDR page procedure timed out -- the remote device did not respond to paging within the page timeout window.
common_causes: Remote device out of range or powered off
Remote device not in page scan mode
Wrong BD_ADDR
RF interference

0x05.name: Authentication Failure
0x05.description: Pairing or authentication failed, typically due to an incorrect PIN, link key, or passkey.
common_causes: Incorrect PIN/passkey entered
Mismatched or stale link keys on one side
User cancelled pairing

0x06.name: PIN or Key Missing
0x06.description: The remote device indicated it has no stored link key or PIN for this peer, so a previously-encrypted link cannot be re-established.
common_causes: Remote device's bonded keys were cleared/factory reset
Local host attempted to reconnect with a stale link key

0x07.name: Memory Capacity Exceeded
0x07.description: The controller has run out of internal memory to track the operation (e.g. too many simultaneous connections or stored keys).
common_causes: Too many simultaneous connections for controller's resources
Bonded-device key storage full

0x08.name: Connection Timeout
0x08.description: An established connection was lost because no valid packets were received within the supervision timeout window.
common_causes: Devices moved out of RF range
Severe interference dropping packets
Peer device crashed or lost power without sending a disconnect
Supervision timeout set too aggressively for the link's connection interval

0x09.name: Connection Limit Exceeded
0x09.description: The controller already has the maximum number of connections it supports and rejected a new one.
common_causes: Application opened more simultaneous BT connections than the controller supports

0x0A.name: Synchronous Connection Limit To A Device Exceeded
0x0A.description: Too many synchronous (SCO/eSCO) connections were requested to the same device.
common_causes: Multiple audio links opened to one peer simultaneously

0x0B.name: Connection Already Exists
0x0B.description: A connection request was made to a device that already has an active connection.
common_causes: Duplicate connect request race from upper layer

0x0C.name: Command Disallowed
0x0C.description: The command cannot be executed because the controller is in a state where it isn't allowed (e.g. wrong mode, already in progress).
common_causes: Command issued out of sequence relative to controller state machine
Overlapping commands of the same type issued back-to-back

0x0D.name: Connection Rejected due to Limited Resources
0x0D.description: The remote device rejected the incoming connection because it lacks resources to accept it.
common_causes: Remote device's connection table is full
Remote device is low on memory/power

0x0E.name: Connection Rejected Due To Security Reasons
0x0E.description: The remote device rejected the connection on security grounds (e.g. it requires authentication/encryption the initiator didn't provide).
common_causes: Peer requires bonding/encryption before allowing the connection
Local device's security mode/IO capability is insufficient for peer's requirements

0x0F.name: Connection Rejected due to Unacceptable BD_ADDR
0x0F.description: The remote device rejected the connection because of the initiator's Bluetooth address (e.g. it isn't on an allow list).
common_causes: Peer is using an accept/allow list that excludes this device's address
Address type mismatch (public vs random) confusing peer's filter

0x10.name: Connection Accept Timeout Exceeded
0x10.description: The local controller's connection accept timeout expired before the connection could complete.
common_causes: Remote stopped responding mid-handshake
Connection accept timeout configured too short for the RF environment

0x11.name: Unsupported Feature or Parameter Value
0x11.description: A requested feature or parameter value is not supported by the controller or remote device.
common_causes: Host requested a feature (e.g. a specific PHY, data length) the controller/peer doesn't implement
Version/capability mismatch between host stack and controller firmware

0x12.name: Invalid HCI Command Parameters
0x12.description: One or more parameters in the HCI command were outside the valid range or otherwise malformed.
common_causes: Host stack bug constructing the command
Parameter values outside spec-defined range (e.g. invalid connection interval)

0x13.name: Remote User Terminated Connection
0x13.description: The remote side's user/application explicitly closed the connection (a normal, intentional disconnect).
common_causes: User closed the app or turned off Bluetooth on the peer device
Peer application logic decided to disconnect

0x14.name: Remote Device Terminated Connection due to Low Resources
0x14.description: The remote device disconnected because it ran low on resources (memory, connection slots, battery management, etc.).
common_causes: Peer's connection/memory limits reached
Peer entering a low-power state and shedding connections

0x15.name: Remote Device Terminated Connection due to Power Off
0x15.description: The remote device disconnected because it is powering off.
common_causes: Peer device shutting down or its battery died
User powered off the peer device

0x16.name: Connection Terminated By Local Host
0x16.description: The local host explicitly requested the disconnect (normal, intentional local disconnect).
common_causes: Application explicitly called disconnect
Local stack tore down the link after an error elsewhere

0x17.name: Repeated Attempts
0x17.description: The controller rejected the request because of repeated unsuccessful attempts in a short window (anti-flood / anti-bruteforce protection).
common_causes: Pairing retried too many times too quickly
Connection retried in a tight loop after failures

0x18.name: Pairing Not Allowed
0x18.description: Pairing was attempted but is not currently allowed by the remote device.
common_causes: Peer not in pairing/discoverable mode
Peer's pairing window already closed

0x19.name: Unknown LMP PDU
0x19.description: The controller received an LMP PDU it does not understand.
common_causes: Firmware/baseband incompatibility between peers

0x1A.name: Unsupported Remote Feature
0x1A.description: The remote device does not support a feature that was requested or required for the procedure.
common_causes: Local stack assumed a feature (e.g. Secure Connections, LE Data Length Extension) the peer doesn't implement

0x1B.name: SCO Offset Rejected
0x1B.description: Remote rejected the requested SCO timing offset.
common_causes: Peer's scheduler cannot accommodate the requested SCO offset

0x1C.name: SCO Interval Rejected
0x1C.description: Remote rejected the requested SCO interval.
common_causes: Peer cannot support the requested audio link interval

0x1D.name: SCO Air Mode Rejected
0x1D.description: Remote rejected the requested SCO air mode (codec/coding format for the voice link).
common_causes: Peer doesn't support the requested audio codec/air mode (e.g. CVSD vs mSBC)

0x1E.name: Invalid LMP Parameters / Invalid LL Parameters
0x1E.description: A parameter in an LMP (BR/EDR) or LL (LE) procedure was invalid or not acceptable.
common_causes: Out-of-range connection parameters requested (interval, latency, timeout)
Stack bug generating malformed link-layer parameters

0x1F.name: Unspecified Error
0x1F.description: An error occurred that doesn't have a more specific error code -- a catch-all failure.
common_causes: Controller-internal failure not otherwise classified
Often paired with vendor-specific debug logs for the real cause

0x20.name: Unsupported LMP Parameter Value / Unsupported LL Parameter Value
0x20.description: A parameter value, while well-formed, isn't supported by this controller.
common_causes: Requested PHY, data length, or connection parameter exceeds controller's supported range

0x21.name: Role Change Not Allowed
0x21.description: A master/slave role switch request was rejected.
common_causes: Peer doesn't support role switching
Role switch attempted at an invalid time (e.g. during an active SCO link)

0x22.name: LMP Response Timeout / LL Response Timeout
0x22.description: A procedure timed out waiting for a peer's link-layer response.
common_causes: RF link too poor for timely LL/LMP responses
Peer firmware hung mid-procedure
Peer disconnected/powered off mid-negotiation

0x23.name: LMP Error Transaction Collision / LL Procedure Collision
0x23.description: Two link-layer procedures collided (both sides initiated conflicting procedures at the same time).
common_causes: Both peers simultaneously initiated a connection-parameter or PHY update procedure

0x24.name: LMP PDU Not Allowed
0x24.description: The LMP PDU received isn't allowed in the controller's current state.
common_causes: Out-of-sequence baseband procedure

0x25.name: Encryption Mode Not Acceptable
0x25.description: The proposed encryption mode/strength is not acceptable to one side.
common_causes: Security policy requires stronger encryption than peer offered
Legacy peer only supports weaker encryption than local policy allows

0x26.name: Link Key cannot be Changed
0x26.description: A request to change the link key was rejected.
common_causes: Unit key in use where a combination key is required

0x27.name: Requested QoS Not Supported
0x27.description: The requested quality-of-service parameters are not supported.
common_causes: Application requested bandwidth/latency the link cannot guarantee

0x28.name: Instant Passed
0x28.description: A link-layer procedure referencing a future connection event ('instant') arrived after that instant had already passed.
common_causes: Connection parameter/PHY/channel-map update applied too late, often due to a slow or congested link
Clock drift between peers

0x29.name: Pairing With Unit Key Not Supported
0x29.description: Pairing using a (deprecated) unit key was attempted but isn't supported.
common_causes: Legacy peer trying to use unit-key pairing against a stack that requires combination keys

0x2A.name: Different Transaction Collision
common_causes: Overlapping unrelated procedures initiated by both sides at once

0x2C.name: QoS Unacceptable Parameter
0x2C.description: QoS parameters proposed were not acceptable.
common_causes: Requested bandwidth/latency outside what the link supports

0x2D.name: QoS Rejected
0x2D.description: The QoS request was rejected outright.
common_causes: Peer cannot support any QoS guarantee for the requested traffic

0x2E.name: Channel Classification Not Supported
0x2E.description: AFH channel classification was requested but isn't supported.
common_causes: Peer controller doesn't implement adaptive frequency hopping channel classification

0x2F.name: Insufficient Security
0x2F.description: The current security level on the link is insufficient for the requested operation.
common_causes: GATT/profile operation requires encryption or authentication that hasn't been established yet
Application accessed a protected attribute before pairing completed

0x30.name: Parameter Out of Mandatory Range
0x30.description: A parameter value is outside the range mandated by the specification.
common_causes: Host requested connection interval/latency/timeout combination outside spec-mandated bounds

0x32.name: Role Switch Pending
0x32.description: A role switch is already pending and a new request collided with it.
common_causes: Role switch requested twice in quick succession

0x34.name: Reserved Slot Violation
0x34.description: The reserved slot for a synchronous connection was violated.
common_causes: Scheduling conflict among multiple synchronous (SCO/eSCO) links

0x35.name: Role Switch Failed
0x35.description: An attempted role switch did not complete successfully.
common_causes: Peer rejected or could not complete the switch mid-procedure

0x36.name: Extended Inquiry Response Too Large
0x36.description: The Extended Inquiry Response data exceeds the supported size.
common_causes: Application wrote EIR data larger than 240 bytes

0x37.name: Secure Simple Pairing Not Supported By Host
0x37.description: The host does not support Secure Simple Pairing, blocking a procedure that requires it.
common_causes: Legacy host stack paired against a peer that mandates SSP

0x38.name: Host Busy - Pairing
0x38.description: The host is already busy with a pairing procedure and cannot start another.
common_causes: Second pairing attempt initiated while the first is still in progress

0x39.name: Connection Rejected due to No Suitable Channel Found
0x39.description: No suitable channel could be found for the connection (AFH-related).
common_causes: Severe RF interference removing too many usable channels

0x3A.name: Controller Busy
0x3A.description: The controller is too busy to process the request right now.
common_causes: Controller resource exhaustion from too many concurrent operations

0x3B.name: Unacceptable Connection Parameters
0x3B.description: The proposed connection parameters (interval, latency, timeout) were rejected as unacceptable.
common_causes: App requested connection interval/latency combination the peer rejects
Connection parameter update collided with peer's own constraints

0x3C.name: Advertising Timeout
0x3C.description: Advertising stopped because its configured timeout/duration expired without a connection.
common_causes: No scanner/central connected within the advertising window
Advertising duration configured too short

0x3D.name: Connection Terminated due to MIC Failure
0x3D.description: The link was torn down because a Message Integrity Check failed on an encrypted packet, indicating the encrypted session is corrupted or compromised.
common_causes: Encryption key mismatch between peers after a reconnect with stale keys
RF corruption flipping bits in an encrypted PDU
Potential man-in-the-middle/replay tampering

0x3E.name: Connection Failed to be Established / Synchronization Timeout
0x3E.description: The connection procedure started but never completed -- the link-layer connection could not be established or synchronization was lost before it finished.
common_causes: Peer moved out of range during the connection setup window
Severe interference during the connection-establishment phase
Peer firmware aborted mid-handshake

0x40.name: Coarse Clock Adjustment Rejected
0x40.description: A coarse clock adjustment was requested but rejected.
common_causes: Peer controller doesn't support coarse clock adjustment

0x41.name: Type0 Submap Not Defined
0x41.description: A Type0 channel submap was referenced but not defined.
common_causes: LE channel selection algorithm configuration error

0x42.name: Unknown Advertising Identifier
0x42.description: A command referenced an advertising set identifier the controller doesn't recognize.
common_causes: Host referenced an advertising handle that was already removed or never created

0x43.name: Limit Reached
0x43.description: An internal limit (e.g. number of advertising sets, periodic sync, or filter list entries) was reached.
common_causes: Application created more LE resources (adv sets, sync handles) than the controller supports

0x44.name: Operation Cancelled by Host
0x44.description: The host explicitly cancelled the in-progress operation.
common_causes: Application called a cancel command (e.g. Create Connection Cancel) before completion

0x45.name: Packet Too Long
0x45.description: A packet exceeded the maximum length supported for this PHY/connection.
common_causes: Data length exceeds what was negotiated or what the PHY supports
